agent.name: Useful Assistant Six
agent.description: A personal AI assistant that's actually helpful. Reads files, searches the web, manages memory, and gets things done without asking unnecessary questions.

_docs._about: This section documents all available manifest.json options. Remove this _docs block before submitting.
_docs.agent.name: Display name for the agent (max 100 chars).
_docs.agent.description: Agent personality or purpose (max 10,000 chars). Shown in the dashboard.
_docs.agent.vibe: Short tagline (max 200 chars). Shown under the agent name.
_docs.agent.emoji: Emoji icon for the agent (max 32 chars).
_docs.template.slug: URL-friendly identifier (lowercase, hyphens only). Used in marketplace URLs.
_docs.template.category: Template category (e.g. general, defi, content, social, devops).
_docs.template.tags: Search tags, max 10. Help users discover your template.
_docs.secrets: Array of secrets the agent needs. Values are stored encrypted, never in the manifest. Each secret has: name (env var name), description (human-readable), required (boolean, default true).
_docs.skills: Array of skills to attach. Each has: cid (IPFS content ID), name (optional, human-readable). Max 20.
_docs.tasks: Scheduled tasks. Each has: name, prompt (sent to agent), schedule (cron expression like '0 9 * * *' OR milliseconds like '600000ms'), enabled (boolean). Max 20.
_docs.scripts.build: Shell command that runs after git push. Use it to install dependencies or compile assets.
_docs.scripts.start: Shell command that runs on agent boot. Use it to start a server or long-running process. Consider using pm2 for process management.
_docs.routes: Port forwarding rules. Each has: port (1025-65535), path (URL prefix like '/dashboard'), protected (boolean, default true = requires auth). Max 10. Your server must bind to 0.0.0.0, not localhost.
_docs.channels.telegram.dmPolicy: 'pairing' (requires pairing code), 'open' (anyone can DM), or 'closed' (no DMs).
_docs.channels.telegram.allowFrom: Array of Telegram user IDs allowed to message.

secrets.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
secrets.description: Anthropic API key for Claude models

scripts.build: cd ./workspace/server && npm install --include=dev && npm run build

tasks.name: health-check
tasks.prompt: Run `curl -sf http://localhost:3000/health` to check if the server is up. If it fails, run `cd /home/node/clawd/workspace/server && ./node_modules/.bin/pm2 resurrect || ./node_modules/.bin/pm2 start ecosystem.config.js` to restart it. Reply with a short status: OK, RESTARTED, or FAILED.
